QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_59

question.text: What is the lock-in period for NPS-TTS (Tier II Tax Saver)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NPS-TTS has a 3-year lock-in period from the date of each contribution to qualify for Section 80C deduction. **The Trap:** The lock-in is 3 years, not 5 years (like ELSS) or 15 years (like PPF). Each contribution has its own 3-year lock-in. **Cross-Exam Context:** NPS-TTS lock-in (3 years) is comparable to ELSS (3 years) but has lower equity allocation (10-25% vs 65%+ for ELSS). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compare NPS-TTS with ELSS for Central Govt clients. NPS-TTS is more conservative with lower equity, suitable for risk-averse investors.
